mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate below 6.0 percent signals tightening labor market conditions that boost consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 6.0 percent within a single month.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling housing starts indicates a structural shift in capital allocation toward manufacturing over residential construction, which precedes equity outperformance as corporate capex accelerates.
failure_condition: The spread between INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict 60-day forward returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 52% over a 10-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth combined with stable consumer credit balances indicates broad-based economic expansion that lifts corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth exceeds 2.5 percent annualized
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5 percent annualized for two consecutive quarters, indicating growth scares dominate earnings optimism.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which directly supports equity valuations by lowering input cost inflation and boosting output volumes.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises for three consecutive months while SPX 5-day forward returns remain negative in more than 60% of those episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 3-month cumulative increase in the federal funds rate above 0.25 percentage points signals aggressive monetary tightening that raises borrowing costs and compresses equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion that drives wage growth and consumer demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 200,000 in a single month
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of months where PAYEMS exceeds 200,000 and UNRATE declines simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts coupled with expanding real GDP growth indicate a synchronized expansion in both residential and commercial investment, which precedes broad-based equity rallies by improving corporate earnings expectations over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of observations where HOUST exceeds 800,000 and GDPC1 grows positively quarter-over-quarter.
